QF.id: Retirement Planning Products: Other Investment Products_1

question.text: Under the EPF scheme, the standard contribution rate for both employee and employer on basic wages plus dearness allowance is:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The EPF Act mandates 12% contribution from both employee and employer on basic wages and dearness allowance. 10% applies only to certain special establishments. **The Trap:** 8.33% is the EPS diversion from the employer's EPF contribution, not the EPF rate itself. Don't confuse EPF contribution with EPS allocation. **Cross-Exam Context:** EPF contribution rates are tested in Series X-B (retirement products) and Series XVII. NPS has different rates (10% employee, 14% govt employer). **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clarify to clients that 12% is the statutory minimum — employees can contribute more through Voluntary Provident Fund (VPF).
